sv.ref: Baldwin — Practical Guide to Sous Vide (core temps); ChefSteps/Serious Eats salmon
sv.note: Salmon rare–medium-rare 43–50°C per Baldwin; 50°C = tender, just-set. ~30–45 min for a 25–30mm fillet.

smoke.note: Low pit ~65–80°C, pull ~52°C for silky texture; fruit wood is classic for salmon.

smoke_only.note: Smoke-only ~70–80°C for ~1.5–2h to core ~52°C.

safe.note: Displayed conservative floor 63°C (FDA 145°F). Salmon served at 50°C is below the pasteurization floor — parasite-freeze (−20°C/7d or −35°C/15h) for raw/rare service.

grill.note: Skin-side down over direct medium-high grate ~230°C, 3–4 min then flip briefly, pull ~50–52°C.

rationale: Kept svt 50°C (Baldwin salmon 43–50°C rare–med-rare). Documented safe floor at 63°C with parasite-freeze note for rare service; grill numbers from Serious Eats/AmazingRibs.

sv.ref: Baldwin fish core temps; ChefSteps/Serious Eats trout
sv.note: Delicate trout ~50°C, ~30 min for a thin (15–20mm) fillet; Baldwin fish med-rare 49°C.

smoke.note: Low pit 65–80°C, pull ~54°C; fruit wood classic for trout.

smoke_only.note: Smoke-only ~70–80°C ~1–1.5h to ~54°C core.

safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; freshwater trout served rare should be parasite-frozen. Farmed trout lower risk but still document freeze for raw service.

grill.note: Whole/butterflied over direct medium ~220°C, ~3 min/side, pull ~54°C.

rationale: Kept svt 50°C (Baldwin fish med-rare). Documented 63°C displayed floor with freeze note; grill/smoke from AmazingRibs seafood.

sv.ref: amazingfoodmadeeasy sous vide shrimp (Logsdon); ChefSteps shrimp
sv.note: Shrimp IS sous-vided: 122°F/50°C sushi-like, 132°F/55.6°C tender, 140°F/60°C firm; chose 57°C tender-firm, 15–35 min. Added real value vs prior svt=0.

smoke.note: Hot finish/smoke-roast ~230°C only a few minutes; shrimp cook fast.

smoke_only.note: Direct hot, ~5–6 min total until opaque.

safe.ref: FDA shellfish cooked to opaque; Baldwin
safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C. Sous-vide shrimp at 57°C is safe when served immediately (Baldwin fish 55–60°C pasteurization range).

grill.note: Skewer, direct high heat ~250°C, ~1.5 min/side to just opaque. Kenji: air-dry + baking soda for snap.

rationale: Added real sous-vide step 57°C/0.5h (shrimp is genuinely sous-vided; source gives 50/55.6/60°C ladder). Kept 63°C displayed floor; grill from Kenji.

sv.ref: amazingfoodmadeeasy sous vide shrimp; ChefSteps shrimp
sv.note: Same as jumbo shrimp: 55.6°C tender / 60°C firm; chose 57°C, 15–35 min. Added value vs prior svt=0.

smoke.note: Hot finish ~230°C, a few minutes.

smoke_only.note: Direct hot ~5–6 min to opaque.

safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; 57°C SV safe served immediately.

grill.note: Skewer, direct high ~250°C, ~1.5 min/side to opaque.

rationale: Added real sous-vide 57°C/0.5h (same shrimp source ladder). Kept 63°C floor; grill from Kenji.

sv.ref: amazingfoodmadeeasy shrimp/prawn; Baldwin fish
sv.note: Large prawns are sous-vided a touch lower/longer than small shrimp: ~54°C (130°F) tender-succulent, ~40–45 min for jumbo size. Added value vs prior svt=0.

smoke.note: Hot finish ~230°C, split-shell, a few minutes.

smoke_only.note: Direct hot split-in-shell ~6–7 min to opaque.

safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; 54°C SV succulent, safe served immediately.

grill.note: Split lengthwise, shell-side down direct ~240°C ~3 min then flesh ~1 min, to opaque.

rationale: Added sous-vide 54°C/0.75h (larger prawn, succulent per source). Kept 63°C floor; grill from Kenji/AmazingRibs.

sv.ref: ChefSteps scallops; Serious Eats/Anova sous vide scallops
sv.note: ChefSteps: 123°F/51°C, 30 min, then hard sear 20–30s/side. Scallops ARE sous-vided; set 50°C, 30 min. Added value vs prior svt=0.

smoke.note: Very hot brief sear to crust; keep center ~50–54°C.

smoke_only.note: Direct very hot, ~2 min/side, do not overcook.

safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C. Scallops customarily served medium (50–54°C center); safe when eaten immediately after SV/sear.

grill.note: Pat very dry, blazing grate ~260°C, ~1.5–2 min/side to golden crust, translucent center ~50°C.

rationale: Added sous-vide 50°C/0.5h (ChefSteps 123°F/30min). Kept 63°C displayed floor and tgt 54; hard-sear finish from ChefSteps.

sv.ref: Serious Eats/Food Lab (Kenji) via Anova; amazingfoodmadeeasy lobster
sv.note: Kenji: 130°F/54.4°C = tender, succulent (120°F soft/translucent, 140°F traditional-steamed). Tail meat sous-vided ~45–60 min. Added value vs prior svt=0.

smoke.note: Split, brief hot finish shell-side then flesh; baste with butter.

smoke_only.note: Split, direct ~230°C ~8–10 min to opaque.

safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; SV tail served at 54°C tender — safe eaten immediately.

grill.note: Butterfly, shell-side down direct ~230°C ~5 min, flip flesh ~2–3 min, baste, pull ~54–60°C.

rationale: Added sous-vide 54°C/0.75h (Kenji Food Lab 130°F tender). Kept 63°C floor; grill/finish from AmazingRibs.

sv.ref: Serious Eats/Food Lab (Kenji) via Anova sous vide lobster
sv.note: Whole lobster: tail best ~54°C but CLAW meat only firms pleasantly ~150°F/66°C — Kenji notes claw needs a different temp. Chose 60°C compromise for whole animal, ~45 min. Added value vs prior svt=0.

smoke.note: Par-cook/split, hot finish ~220°C basting with butter.

smoke_only.note: Split whole, direct ~220°C ~12 min to opaque.

safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; claw meat wants ~66°C for texture (Kenji), tail lower.

grill.note: Parboil briefly, split, shell-side direct ~220°C then flesh, baste, ~10–12 min total.

rationale: Added sous-vide 60°C/0.75h as whole-animal compromise (Kenji: tail 54°C, claw needs ~66°C). Kept 63°C floor; grill from AmazingRibs.

sv.ref: Serious Eats/Anova shellfish; amazingfoodmadeeasy (prawn/lobster analog)
sv.note: Delicate langoustine (small clawed lobster) sous-vided gently ~50°C, ~20–30 min — like lobster's soft-translucent-to-tender band; overcooks fast. Added value vs prior svt=0.

smoke.note: Split, very brief hot finish; tiny tails cook in minutes.

smoke_only.note: Split, direct hot ~5 min to opaque.

safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; served ~50°C tender, safe eaten immediately.

grill.note: Split, shell-side down direct ~240°C ~2 min then flesh ~1 min, baste.

rationale: Added sous-vide 50°C/0.5h (delicate, lobster-analog soft-tender band). Kept 63°C floor; grill from AmazingRibs.

sv.ref: AmazingRibs/America's Test Kitchen grilled crab; ChefSteps
sv.note: KEPT svt=0: whole blue crab is steamed/steam-grilled live, not sous-vided — no meaningful bag cook for a whole hard-shell crab. Cook by steam over a water pan on the grill 10–15 min until hot.

smoke.note: Steam-grill over water pan, indirect medium ~210°C, 10–15 min until shell bright and hot.

smoke_only.note: Steam-grill ~210°C 10–15 min.

safe.ref: FDA — cook crab until internal 145°F/63°C and flesh opaque
safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; crab should reach opaque/pearly white throughout.

grill.ref: ATK gas-grilled crab; blue crab grill method
grill.note: Water pan on direct side to steam, crabs on indirect side, lid down 10–15 min until hot.

rationale: Kept svt=0 — whole blue crab is a steam/steam-grill item, no genuine sous-vide use. Documented 63°C cooked floor and grill steam method (ATK/AmazingRibs).

sv.ref: Alaskan King Crab Co.; ChefSteps low-temp crab
sv.note: KEPT svt=0: king crab legs are sold pre-cooked/flash-frozen — the task is reheat-through, done by steam/grill/oven, not a sous-vide cook. No raw-protein SV step.

smoke.note: Brush with butter/oil, grill ~150°C (300°F) ~5 min/side just to heat through; already cooked.

smoke_only.note: Reheat ~5 min/side to hot; do not overcook (dries out).

safe.ref: FDA — reheat pre-cooked crab to 145°F/63°C hot
safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; product is pre-cooked, reheat to steaming/hot.

grill.note: Split legs, brush butter, ~150–260°C grate ~5 min/side or foil-pack indirect 10 min just to heat.

rationale: Kept svt=0 — legs come pre-cooked; only reheating is needed (steam/grill), not sous-vide. Documented 63°C reheat floor and grill method.

sv.ref: Serious Eats soft-shell crab; AmazingRibs seafood
sv.note: KEPT svt=0: soft-shell crab is a fast dry-heat item (pan-fry/grill until crisp) — the appeal is a crisp shell, which sous-vide would defeat. No SV step used.

smoke.note: Direct hot ~230°C, ~3–4 min/side until crisp and opaque.

smoke_only.note: Direct hot ~7–8 min total to crisp/opaque.

safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; cook until firm and opaque throughout.

grill.note: Clean, brush oil, direct ~240°C ~3–4 min/side, top-shell first, until crisp.

rationale: Kept svt=0 — soft-shell crab is a crisp dry-heat fry/grill item; sous-vide is not used and would ruin the crisp shell. Documented 63°C floor and direct-grill numbers.

sv.ref: ChefSteps tender-silky octopus; Thomas Keller Under Pressure (77°C/5h)
sv.note: Octopus IS sous-vided long/hot to convert collagen: 77°C (170.6°F) for 5h (Keller); alt 76.6°C/4–8h. Added real value vs prior svt=0.

smoke.note: After tenderizing, char over very hot fire ~10 min for crisp edges; oak/vine wood Mediterranean-style.

smoke_only.note: Note: without SV, octopus must be simmer/braise-tenderized first, THEN charred ~10 min — dry high heat alone will not tenderize.

safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; octopus cooked well past that (77°C) for texture, not safety.

grill.note: Pre-tenderize (SV 77°C/5h), pat dry, blazing grate ~260°C ~2–3 min/side for char; internal already ~75–77°C.

rationale: Added real sous-vide 77°C/5h (ChefSteps/Keller collagen conversion) then hot char. Kept 63°C displayed floor and tgt 75; grill char from AmazingRibs/Serious Eats.

sv.ref: amazingfoodmadeeasy sous vide squid
sv.note: Squid IS sous-vided to tenderize: 138°F/59°C for 2–3h (or 180°F/82°C 1h). 138°F most reliable for tender calamari; check hourly (over-long = mushy). Added value vs prior svt=0. Flash-grill remains a valid alternative.

smoke.note: Blistering hot, very brief — squid cooks in ~2 min; longer = rubbery.

smoke_only.note: Flash direct very hot ~2–3 min total, OR the SV-tenderize route for large/tough squid.

safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; squid safe either flash-cooked hot or SV-tenderized.

grill.note: Score, oil, blazing grate ~260°C ~1 min/side to just opaque with char; do NOT overcook.

rationale: Added sous-vide 59°C/2h tenderize (source: 138°F most reliable for tender calamari) with flash-grill as the alternative. Kept 63°C floor; grill from AmazingRibs/Serious Eats.

sv.ref: Serious Eats/Food Lab tuna via amazingfoodmadeeasy; Kenji 115°F
sv.note: Tuna IS sous-vided rare: 115°F/46°C moist & translucent (rare); 120°F/49°C firm/dry. Set 45°C rare, 25–45 min for 25mm. Added value vs prior svt=0.

smoke.note: Sear the exterior only, rare center ~45°C; a few seconds/side over blazing heat.

smoke_only.note: Blazing hot ~30–45s/side, rare center.

safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C. Tuna served rare at 45°C is FAR below the pasteurization floor — use sushi/sashimi-grade, parasite-frozen (−20°C/7d or −35°C/15h) fish; center is not pasteurized.

grill.note: Oil, blistering grate ~290°C, ~30–45s/side for a rare (45°C) center with grill-marked crust.

rationale: Added sous-vide 45°C/0.5h rare (Kenji 115°F/46°C rare). Kept 63°C displayed floor with explicit sashimi-grade + parasite-freeze note since 45°C center is unpasteurized.

sv.ref: Serious Eats/reluctantgourmet + amazingfoodmadeeasy swordfish
sv.note: Swordfish IS sous-vided: 125–130°F sweet spot (125°F/52°C tender-juicy, 130°F/54°C firm-flaky). Set 54°C, 30–45 min. Added value vs prior svt=0.

smoke.note: Meaty steak, brief hot finish; keep from drying (lean-firm fish).

smoke_only.note: Direct medium-high ~230°C ~4–5 min/side to just-firm.

safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; SV swordfish served ~54°C moist — parasite-freeze if serving rare.

grill.note: Firm meaty steak takes direct high ~250°C well, ~3–4 min/side, pull ~52–54°C to stay moist.

rationale: Added sous-vide 54°C/0.75h (source sweet-spot 125–130°F). Kept 63°C floor and tgt 60; grill from AmazingRibs/Serious Eats.

sv.ref: Serious Eats/Anova + amazingfoodmadeeasy halibut
sv.note: Halibut IS sous-vided: 120°F/49°C sashimi-like, 130°F/54.4°C very moist & flaky, 140°F/60°C firm at cusp of tough. Set 52°C (lean fish dries fast), 30–45 min. Added value vs prior svt=0.

smoke.note: Lean/delicate — gentle finish, watch closely, keep moist.

smoke_only.note: Direct-medium ~210°C ~4 min/side to just-flaky; overcooks easily.

safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; SV halibut served ~52°C is below pasteurization — parasite-freeze for rare service.

grill.note: Lean and prone to sticking/drying — well-oiled grate, two-zone, ~220°C ~3 min/side, pull ~52–54°C.

rationale: Added sous-vide 52°C/0.5h (source 130°F very-moist band, chosen slightly lower for lean halibut). Kept 63°C floor and tgt 58; two-zone grill from AmazingRibs.

sv.ref: AmazingRibs smoke-catcher clambake; ATK grilled mussels
sv.note: KEPT svt=0: mussels are opened by live heat/steam on the grate — they pop open when done. No sous-vide; a SV bag would not open the shells or give the smoke.

smoke.note: Direct heat, covered, until shells open: ~4–6 min for mussels. Toss wood chips for smoke.

smoke_only.note: Direct hot ~4–6 min until they pop open; discard any that stay shut.

safe.ref: FDA — cook shellfish until shells open and flesh is done
safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; safety cue for bivalves is the shell opening. Discard unopened.

grill.note: Place on grate curved-side down, level, direct heat, lid down, ~4–6 min until open; spoon butter, let some hit fire for smoke.

rationale: Kept svt=0 — mussels are a live-fire/steam bivalve opened by heat, no sous-vide step exists. Documented 63°C floor (shell-opening cue) and grill method (AmazingRibs/ATK).

sv.ref: AmazingRibs smoke-catcher clambake; ATK grilled clams
sv.note: KEPT svt=0: clams open on the grate from live heat/steam — no sous-vide. Purge in ~4% saltwater first, then grill until they pop open.

smoke.note: Direct heat, covered, ~7–10 min until clams open (longer than mussels).

smoke_only.note: Direct hot ~7–10 min until open; discard any that stay shut.

safe.ref: FDA — cook shellfish until shells open
safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C; shell-opening is the doneness cue. Discard unopened.

grill.note: Purged clams on grate, direct heat, lid down, ~7–10 min until open; keep level so liquor doesn't spill, spoon butter for smoke flare.

rationale: Kept svt=0 — clams open by live heat/steam, no sous-vide step. Documented 63°C floor (shell-opening) and purge+grill method (AmazingRibs/ATK).

sv.ref: AmazingRibs seafood; ATK grilled oysters
sv.note: KEPT svt=0: oysters are grilled/roasted over live fire until they pop open (or on the half-shell with butter) — no sous-vide. Served raw or fire-roasted, never bag-cooked.

smoke.note: Direct heat, cupped-side down, ~4–6 min until they open/edges curl; oak smoke.

smoke_only.note: Direct hot ~4–6 min until open; or half-shell with butter until bubbling.

safe.ref: FDA — cook shellfish until shells open (raw oysters carry Vibrio risk)
safe.note: Displayed floor 63°C. Raw-served oysters must be from certified live/harvest-tagged stock; cooking to open kills Vibrio.

grill.note: Cupped-side down on grate curved to hold liquor, direct heat, ~4–6 min until they pop open / edges curl; add butter, let it flare for smoke.

rationale: Kept svt=0 — oysters are fire-roasted/opened by heat (or served raw), no sous-vide. Documented 63°C floor with Vibrio/raw-service note and grill method (AmazingRibs/ATK).
